_comment: Sunday Batch Orchestrator blueprints (PRD R7–R9): parallel-track timelines across stations. Seed = one short demo blueprint so M1 has data to render; real multi-recipe blueprints are authored content (out of build-loop scope).

tasks.text: Lime & marinate the chicken; chop tomato, onion, garlic, pepper, carrots.
Heat oil and brown the chicken in batches.
tasks.while_waiting: While each batch browns, mix the cornstarch into the coconut milk.

tasks.text: Return chicken, add marinade + carrots, cook 10 min.
Stir in the coconut-milk slurry; simmer until glossy and chicken reads 165°F.
Preheat the oven to 400°F and grease the muffin tin.
Brown 8 oz sausage for the egg bites (~6 min); drain and cool.
tasks.while_waiting: While the sausage browns, whisk the eggs + cream for the bites.

tasks.text: Roast the Cobb chicken breast 22–25 min until 165°F; rest and slice.
tasks.while_waiting: While the chicken roasts, fill the muffin cups with the egg mixture.

tasks.text: Stir sausage, spinach, and cheddar into the eggs; fill the muffin cups ¾ full.
Bake the egg bites 18–20 min until set; cool and lift out.
tasks.while_waiting: While they bake, hard-boil eggs and crisp the bacon for the Cobb jars.

tasks.text: Hard-boil eggs: boil, turn off heat, cover 12 min, then ice-bath and peel.
Assemble the Cobb jars: dressing, chicken, egg, bacon, blue cheese, tomatoes, romaine on top.
Whisk the chia pudding (chia + almond milk + vanilla + sweetener).
tasks.while_waiting: While the chia sets, start thawing the shrimp in cold water.

tasks.text: Thaw and dry 1 lb shrimp (cold-water soak 15 min, then pat very dry).
Whisk the chia again to break up clumps; divide into 5 jars and chill overnight.
Sauté the lunch shrimp ~2 min/side until pink and curled into a C; chill.
Pack the shrimp & avocado boxes (shrimp, romaine, tomatoes, dressing on the side, lime).
Preheat the oven to 400°F and grease the muffin tin.
Sauté ½ diced onion and 1 diced pepper for the egg muffins 4 min until soft; cool.
tasks.while_waiting: While the veg cools, whisk the eggs + cream and dice the ham.

tasks.text: Hard-boil eggs for the chef salad jars: boil, turn off heat, cover 12 min, then ice-bath and peel.
Stir the veg, ham, and cheddar into the eggs; fill the muffin cups ¾ full.
Bake the egg muffins 18–20 min until set (~160°F); cool 5 min and lift out.
tasks.while_waiting: While they bake, chop the tomatoes and portion the deli ham and turkey.

tasks.text: Assemble the chef salad jars: ranch on the bottom, then ham, turkey, egg, cheddar, tomatoes, romaine on top.
Preheat the oven to 375°F and grease the muffin tin.
Whisk 10 eggs + ¼ cup cream + salt; fold in the finely chopped broccoli and cheddar.
tasks.while_waiting: While you whisk, finely chop the broccoli.

tasks.text: Fill the muffin cups ¾ full.
Bake the mini quiches 20 min until set (~160°F); cool and lift out.
tasks.while_waiting: While they bake, roll the turkey around the cheddar and slice the cucumber for the antipasto boxes.

tasks.text: Pack the antipasto boxes: turkey-and-cheddar rolls, cucumber, peppers, olives, and ranch on the side.
Preheat the oven to 400°F and grease the muffin tin.
Whisk 10 eggs + ¼ cup cream + salt; fold in 3 oz spinach and ¾ cup feta. Fill the cups ¾ full.
Bake the egg bites 18 min until set (~160°F); cool and lift out.
tasks.while_waiting: While they bake, cook the chicken for the Greek salad boxes.

tasks.text: Cook 8 oz chicken for the salad boxes; let it cool, then slice.
Pack the Greek chicken salad boxes: chicken, romaine, cucumber, tomatoes, feta, olives — oil + vinegar on the side.
Soak the wooden skewers for Monday's chicken (30 min in water) so they're ready for the week.
tasks.while_waiting: While the skewers soak, mix tomorrow's tzatziki if you like to get ahead.

tasks.text: Cook 8 oz chicken for the caprese boxes; let it cool, then slice.
tasks.while_waiting: While the chicken cooks, layer the yogurt parfait jars.

tasks.text: Layer 5 parfait jars: ¾ cup Greek yogurt + 2 tbsp walnuts + 1 tsp flax each. Chill (add berries morning-of).
Pack the caprese & chicken boxes: chicken, fresh mozzarella, tomatoes, basil — balsamic + oil on the side.
Move a 1 lb bag of frozen shrimp to the fridge to thaw for Wednesday's lemon garlic shrimp.
Mix the tikka marinade (yogurt, garam masala, cumin, paprika, garlic, ginger, lemon, salt) and coat 1.5 lb chicken; refrigerate to marinate for Monday.
tasks.while_waiting: While the chicken marinates, dice a bell pepper for the breakfast bowls.

tasks.text: Render 8 oz chorizo + 1 diced pepper 6 min until 160°F, then scramble in 8 beaten eggs until just set.
tasks.while_waiting: While the chorizo renders, beat the eggs and shred the cheddar.

tasks.text: Stir cheddar into the scramble and portion into 5 breakfast containers.
Brown 1 lb beef with taco seasoning until 160°F; cool.
tasks.while_waiting: While the beef cools, layer salsa into the lunch jars.

tasks.text: Build the taco salad jars: salsa, beef, cheddar, romaine on top (add avocado morning-of).
Whisk each chia cup (chia + almond milk + cocoa + peanut butter + sweetener) and chill 5 jars overnight.
tasks.while_waiting: While you whisk the cups, set the frozen shrimp in cold water to thaw for the week.

tasks.text: Thaw and dry 1 lb shrimp for Wednesday's fajita bowls (cold-water soak 15 min, then pat very dry).
Toss 4 oz shredded cooked chicken with buffalo sauce.
Pack the buffalo chicken boxes over romaine + celery with blue cheese; ranch on the side.
